misses.why: The multiple-testing haircut only runs when a `study` block is declared. A producer who omits it -- or never knew to -- escapes the check. calma verifies what the contract declares; it does not infer an undeclared flaw. (Mitigation: calma draft/onboard proposes the blocks; the producer must adopt them.)

calma recomputes the metric from the produced outputs; it cannot know the ground-truth labels are themselves wrong. Verifying that a number is correctly computed is not validating the truth of its inputs -- a different problem (data provenance), out of scope by design.
